title: Rocky road
subtitle: Dark chocolate traybake with marshmallows, biscuits, and glacé cherries
excerpt: A no-bake chocolate traybake: broken digestive biscuits, marshmallows, and glacé cherries bound in a dark chocolate and golden syrup base, set in a tin and cut into squares. Makes about 24.
sourceNotes: Rocky road is a confectionery traybake combining chocolate with marshmallows, biscuits, and dried fruit. The combination of chocolate with marshmallows is documented in American confectionery from the early twentieth century — the name is documented from the 1920s. The British form, using digestive biscuits and glacé cherries, is documented in British household baking books from the mid-twentieth century and became a staple of home baking guides and charity recipe collections.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: Keeps refrigerated for 1 week. Bring to room temperature before cutting.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: No-bake. Melt chocolate mixture gently over low heat.

glossaryTerms.definition: Rocky road sets by the chocolate returning to solid at room temperature, not by cooking. The chocolate mixture must be warm enough to coat all the ingredients when it is poured in, but not so hot that it melts the marshmallows. The mixture should be thick and glossy when poured into the tin. The surface will look uneven — the unevenness is the rocky road. Refrigerate until firm, usually 1–2 hours. If cut straight from the fridge, the chocolate will snap cleanly; at room temperature it cuts with more give.

content.text: Rocky road is assembled, not baked. The skill is in the melt: the chocolate base should be poured in while still fluid but not hot enough to dissolve the marshmallows. Once everything is coated, the
takes care of itself in the fridge.

items.prepNote: broken into rough pieces — not crumbed
mini or large cut in half

content.text: Line a 30 × 20 cm tin with baking paper. Melt the {{dark-chocolate}}, {{unsalted-butter}}, and {{golden-syrup}} together in a saucepan over very low heat, stirring until smooth. Remove from the heat and leave to cool for 3–4 minutes — it should be fluid but no longer steaming. Add the {{digestive-biscuit}} pieces, {{marshmallows}}, {{glace-cherries}}, and {{walnuts}} if using, and fold until everything is coated.

Pour into the prepared tin and press down gently so the surface is rough and uneven. Dust with {{cocoa-powder}} if using. Refrigerate for at least 2 hours until fully set. Lift out on the paper, transfer to a board, and cut into squares with a sharp knife. Keep refrigerated.
